methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $94M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $400M - $2.0B.

data.narrative:
LeoLabs is a Series B-stage space and aerospace company founded by Dan Ceperley that has raised $94M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 3 same-stage private comparables (Cowboy Space Corporation at $2.0B, Aalyria at $1.3B, and Hubble Network at $400M) and 5 public space and aerospace companies (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL), though the public set cannot be directly applied due to unavailable revenue data.

Two triangulation methods produced quantifiable results. The stage-progression method applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $179M from the $94M raised proxy. The peer-median approach anchors to the median valuation of $1.3B across the three same-sector, same-stage comparables, with a range spanning $400M to $2.0B. These divergent results reflect substantial heterogeneity within the peer set—a recognized challenge in valuing early-stage aerospace companies where business model maturity and capital requirements vary widely.

The triangulated analysis supports a range of $152M to $1.5B, with a median of $739M. The wide dispersion reflects genuine uncertainty: the stage-progression floor assumes modest growth from current capitalization, while the peer-median ceiling reflects peer-company risk profiles and market dynamics that may not directly apply to LeoLabs. Resolution hinges on visibility into revenue traction, unit economics, and competitive positioning relative to the named peer set.
